title: dactylis glomerata subsp. merinoana
intro: Dactylis glomerata subsp. merinoana, commonly known as Merino orchard grass, is a subspecies of the perennial grass Dactylis glomerata. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Merino orchard grass include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in tinctures and salves.
